response: The internet originated in the 1960s as a U.S. Department of Defense research project, with ARPANET debuting in 1969 and the adoption of TCP/IP protocols in 1983 establishing the foundation for the modern network. Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web at CERN in 1989, and the subsequent launch of the Mosaic browser in 1993, along with the late-1990s dot-com boom, democratized access and created tech giants like Amazon and Google despite the 2001 crash. The mid-2000s brought further transformation through social media platforms, mobile internet access after the 2007 iPhone launch, and cloud computing via AWS in 2006. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people and underpins virtually every aspect of modern life, from commerce and communication to entertainment.
